---
title: "LRPGF02b: Factors associated with gains in receptive vocabulary (R), treated-only"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPGF02b is a treated-only **gain-factors** model for receptive vocabulary (`rowpvt`): what is associated with how much children gain across the three period transitions (ANCOVA - the period post-count given its pre-count - Beta-Binomial logit, child random intercept). It conditions on the own baseline, age, cognitive ability (block design), the upstream DAG skill taught receptive vocabulary (TR), and the revised-DAG confounders hearing (HS = `hs`) and phonological memory (RW = `erbto`) — each with a missing-indicator — with focal interactions group$\times$ability, group$\times$own-baseline, age$\times$ability.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling (DAG guardrails)"}

Once restricted to on-intervention rows the treatment indicator is constant, so it and its interactions are dropped: every coefficient here is an **adjusted association** among the treated (confounded by latent GA), never a treatment effect. Intervention sessions/dose are never conditioned on (a DAG collider). SES is excluded (not a DAG node; statistically redundant).

:::

## Model

Beta-Binomial$(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$ on the period post-count, $N = 170$, with
$\operatorname{logit}\mu = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{period}} + \gamma_{\text{own}}\operatorname{logit}_*(\text{pre}) + \gamma_A A_{\text{std}} + \gamma_{\text{ability}}z(\text{blocks}) + \sum_{s\in\{TR\}}\gamma_s\operatorname{logit}_*(s_{\text{pre}}) + \sum_{c}\gamma_c\,z(c) + \sum\text{interactions} + u_{\text{child}}$, where the $z(c)$ are the standardised revised-DAG confounders hearing (HS = `hs`) and phonological memory (RW = `erbto`), each with a missing-indicator. Priors: shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_factors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
